pattern: Splitting the running total of the first n integers into a divisible-by-m part and everything else is just an arithmetic-series computation on the multiples of m, so no loop over the range is actually needed.
hints: You don't need to inspect every integer from 1 to n individually to separate the two groups.
The integers divisible by m in that range are exactly m, 2m, 3m, ... up to the largest multiple that doesn't exceed n, and their count and sum both follow directly from how many multiples fit.
Compute the total of everything from 1 to n with the usual closed-form sum, compute the divisible-by-m portion the same way, and the answer is the total minus twice the divisible portion.

edge_inputs.description: the smallest possible n and m, where the only integer is divisible by itself
the smallest n with a divisor that never divides it
n and m both at the maximum constraint and equal to each other
n at the maximum with every integer divisible by m
n at the maximum with a divisor that is one less than it
m equal to n, so exactly one integer in the range is divisible by it
a divisor with no clean relationship to n, forcing the general multiple-counting formula
m larger than n, so nothing in the range is divisible by it

stress.description: random n and m pairs across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 1000)))
    m = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [n, m]

stress.description: n fixed at the maximum with every possible divisor exercised across seeds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = 1000
    m = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [n, m]
